---
title: "Annulus background"
---

This notebook estimates a local background from a circular annulus and subtracts
it from exact aperture sums.

```{python}
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import astroapers as aap
```

## Synthetic image with a sloped background

```{python}
ny, nx = 60, 60
y, x = np.mgrid[:ny, :nx]

x0, y0 = 34.7, 33.9
background = 20.0 + 0.03 * x - 0.02 * y
source = 10.0 * np.exp(-0.5 * ((x - x0) ** 2 + (y - y0) ** 2) / 3.2**2)
data = background + source
```

## Source aperture and sky annulus

Use exact fractional weights for the source sum, and center-selected annulus
pixels for robust background samples.

```{python}
ap = aap.CircAp((x0, y0), r=5.0)
an = aap.CircAn((x0, y0), r_in=9.0, r_out=15.0)

apsum, src_npix = ap.apsum(data)
sky_weights = an.weights(method="center")[0]
sky_bbox = an.bboxes()[0]
sky_values = sky_bbox.weighted_values(sky_weights, data)
# you can do complicated math to get best sky value.
# Here I just use median:
sky_median = np.median(sky_values)

# sky subtraction
srcsum = apsum - src_npix * sky_median

# apsum, src_npix, sky_values.size, sky_median, srcsum
print(f"Aperture sum: {apsum:.3f}")
print(f"Sky median: {sky_median:.3f}")
print(f"Source npix: {src_npix:.3f}")
print(f"Source sum: {srcsum:.3f} = apsum - npix * sky")
```

## Visual check

```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(7, 4))
ax[0].imshow(data, origin="lower", cmap="viridis")
ap.plot(ax=ax[0], color="r", lw=1)
an.plot(ax=ax[0], color="w", lw=1)
ax[0].set_title("Source aperture and sky annulus")

# center weights are 0/1, so the weighted_values are just the selected pixels:
skyvals = sky_bbox.weighted_values(sky_weights, data)
ax[1].hist(skyvals, bins=20, color="gray", edgecolor="k")
ax[1].set_title("Sky values")
ax[1].axvline(sky_median, color="r", ls="--", label=f"Median={sky_median:.2f}")
ax[1].legend()

plt.show()
```

## A masked-pixel example

Bad-pixel masks are full image-shaped boolean arrays where `True` means
excluded.

```{python}
bad_pixels = np.zeros(data.shape, dtype=bool)
bad_pixels[40:45, 45:52] = True

sky_values_masked = sky_bbox.weighted_values(sky_weights, data, mask=bad_pixels)
sky_median_masked = np.median(sky_values_masked)
apsum_masked, src_npix_masked = ap.apsum(data, mask=bad_pixels)
srcsum_masked = apsum_masked - src_npix_masked * sky_median_masked

print(f"Unmasked sky pixels: {sky_values.size}")
print(f"Masked sky pixels: {sky_values_masked.size}")
print(f"Unmasked sky median: {sky_median:.3f}")
print(f"Masked sky median: {sky_median_masked:.3f}")
print(f"Masked source npix: {src_npix_masked:.3f}")
print(f"Masked source sum: {srcsum_masked:.3f}")
```

```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(5, 4))
ax.imshow(data, origin="lower", cmap="viridis")
ax.imshow(bad_pixels, origin="lower", cmap="gray_r", alpha=0.4)
ap.plot(ax=ax, color="r", lw=1)
an.plot(ax=ax, color="w", lw=1)
ax.set_title("Bad pixels excluded from aperture and annulus")
plt.show()
```

## Bbox-Tight Weight Helpers

For large images or production-level pipelines, use bbox-tight weights together
with their matching `BoundingBox` objects. This avoids making full image-shaped
float masks and keeps the work restricted to the aperture and annulus bounding
boxes.

```{python}
src_weights = ap.weights(method="exact")[0]
src_bbox = ap.bboxes()[0]
sky_weights = an.weights(method="center")[0]
sky_bbox = an.bboxes()[0]

raw_apsum, raw_src_npix = src_bbox.apsum(src_weights, data, mask=bad_pixels)
raw_sky_values = sky_bbox.weighted_values(sky_weights, data, mask=bad_pixels)
raw_sky_median = np.median(raw_sky_values)
raw_srcsum = raw_apsum - raw_src_npix * raw_sky_median

assert np.allclose(raw_apsum, apsum_masked)
assert np.allclose(raw_src_npix, src_npix_masked)
assert np.allclose(raw_sky_values, sky_values_masked)
assert np.allclose(raw_srcsum, srcsum_masked)

print(f"Low-level source sum: {raw_srcsum:.3f}")
```

If you only need a boolean mask from bbox-tight weights, fill the overlapping
slice explicitly:

```{python}
sky_bpm = np.zeros(data.shape, dtype=bool)
overlap = sky_bbox.overlap_slices(data.shape)
if overlap is not None:
    data_slices, weight_slices = overlap
    sky_bpm[data_slices] = sky_weights[weight_slices] > 0

assert np.array_equal(sky_bpm, sky_bbox.to_image(sky_weights, data.shape) > 0)
```

## Low-Level Kernels for Lowest Overhead

The direct kernels are the lowest-overhead entry point for source aperture sums,
and they accept `mask=bad_pixels` when bad pixels must be excluded. The object
methods for these supported unmasked sums call the same kernels, so direct calls
mostly save Python object overhead; the relative gain is largest for small
aperture counts and can shrink to a near tie for large batches. For the annulus
background, the annulus sum is not the useful quantity: the median needs the
center-selected annulus sample values. Use the low-level center weights with
`BoundingBox.weighted_values()` for that part.

```{python}
import astroapers.kernels as aapk

xpos = np.array([x0])
ypos = np.array([y0])

kernel_src_apsum, kernel_src_npix = aapk.apsum_circ_exact(
    data,
    xpos,
    ypos,
    r=5.0,
    mask=bad_pixels,
)
sky_weights, sky_boxes = aapk.weights_circ_ann_center(
    xpos,
    ypos,
    9.0,
    15.0,
)
kernel_sky_values = sky_boxes[0].weighted_values(
    sky_weights[0],
    data,
    mask=bad_pixels,
)
kernel_sky_median = np.median(kernel_sky_values)
kernel_srcsum = kernel_src_apsum[0] - kernel_src_npix[0] * kernel_sky_median
kernel_sum_only = aapk.apsum_circ_exact(
    data,
    xpos,
    ypos,
    r=5.0,
    mask=bad_pixels,
    return_npix=False,
)

assert np.allclose(kernel_src_apsum[0], apsum_masked)
assert np.allclose(kernel_src_npix[0], src_npix_masked)
assert np.allclose(kernel_sum_only[0], apsum_masked)
assert np.allclose(kernel_sky_values, sky_values_masked)
assert np.allclose(kernel_sky_median, sky_median_masked)
assert np.allclose(kernel_srcsum, srcsum_masked)

kernel_sky_median, sky_median_masked, kernel_srcsum
```

The direct circular-annulus `apsum` function is still useful when you actually
want the weighted annulus flux or mean. It is not a substitute for robust
background sampling:

```{python}
kernel_ann_apsum, kernel_ann_npix = aapk.apsum_circ_ann_exact(
    data,
    xpos,
    ypos,
    r_in=9.0,
    r_out=15.0,
    mask=bad_pixels,
)

kernel_ann_mean = kernel_ann_apsum[0] / kernel_ann_npix[0]
kernel_ann_mean, kernel_sky_median
```
